RedEye Aiming System

Aim with
your eye.
Not the barrel.

Future Optek's RedEye augmented reality glasses deliver target acquisition in under a millisecond — no optic alignment required. Two issued US patents. Proven in live-fire testing. Now extending to broader IVAS, networked and counter-sUAS applications.

The Problem
Traditional optics are a liability in combat

Every second spent aligning eye to optic is a second the threat is already acting. Conventional sights demand posture, alignment and line-of-sight — none of which survive real combat conditions.

  • Require precise eye-to-optic alignment
  • Degrade situational awareness in dynamic environments
  • Fail in low-light and obscured conditions
  • Restrict mobility and body position under fire
  • Useless when engaging from behind cover or non-standard positions
Our Solution
RedEye AR Glasses
01
Eye-Native Aiming
RedEye displays a digital aiming reticle within AR glasses, superimposed over the user's natural field of view. The reticle moves dynamically in real time, responding to changes in orientation of the user's head and firearm to accurately show where the barrel is pointing.
02
100% Passive System
No cameras, no laser emitters, no radio transmissions. RedEye is completely undetectable — it cannot be jammed, detected or spoofed in the field.
03
Any Firearm, Any Position
The compact sensor mounts to any Picatinny or Weaver rail and can be switched between firearms in seconds without re-zeroing. Enables aiming from the hip, behind cover and in vehicles — positions impossible with conventional optics.
04
Counter-sUAS and IVAS Ready
Patent 2 extends the platform to networked multi-device systems, small unmanned aerial system (sUAS) detection and countermeasures, drone-linked sensing and full military-grade IVAS architecture.
How It Works
Three steps. One decisive advantage.
01
Sensor Reads the Firearm
A proprietary IMU sensor module attaches to any Picatinny rail and captures real-time weapon orientation data at sub-millisecond latency and 0.13 mrad precision. No emitters. No cameras.
02
Mixed Reality Places the Dot
The RedEye glasses compute the barrel-to-eye offset in real time and project an aiming reticle exactly where the weapon is pointing — within the shooter's natural field of view. No alignment needed.
03
Target Acquired. Faster.
The shooter sees, aims and engages without breaking posture, ducking to an optic or losing situational awareness. Effective behind cover, on the move, at the hip and in low light.
The Product
Rapid target acquisition.
Even against fast–moving threats.
Intellectual Property
Extensive, protected IP

Future Optek has been granted two patents covering the RedEye technology, protecting every layer of the system — from core sensor fusion hardware through to networked, multi-device, counter-drone and IVAS configurations.

US 11,922,586 B1 — Issued March 5, 2024
Firearm Sight Improvements Using OLED or LCoS Arrays
Projects a stable, daylight-visible reticle into the user's natural field of view via a compact AR waveguide, eliminating the need to align eye to optic to target. The reticle is rendered by the glasses, not a weapon-mounted optic, enabling novel aiming postures and faster target acquisition.
  • Always-on reticle in the FOV — no eye relief constraints
  • No traditional zeroing ritual — alignment from system calibration
  • Head-up situational awareness — FOV not tunnelled through glass
  • Covers pistol and long-gun configurations
View Patent
US 12,380,657 B1 — Issued August 5, 2025
Advanced Networking, Detection and Multi-Device Data Visualisation
Defines a framework for networking multiple devices — including MR glasses, drones and sensing platforms — to capture, fuse and visualise data directly in mixed reality. Describes a modular MR hardware architecture, multi-drone coordination for scanning and mapping, and diverse imaging and detection sensors with edge computing and AI delivering shared situational awareness across users and devices.
  • Shared situational picture across operators, drones and sensors
  • Modular headset supports swappable sensors, radios and compute
  • Networked drone formations for 3D structure scans and recon
  • Broad applicability: defense, public safety, robotics and guidance systems
View Patent

Next-Gen Prototype — Q1 2026
Great leaps forward

Three successive sensor generations have each delivered step-change improvements in accuracy and latency — approaching and now exceeding military-grade IVAS specification thresholds.

1.22 mrad
Phase I: Sensor refresh at <10ms latency. Core concept validated under live-fire conditions.
0.49 mrad
Phase II: New sensor stack at <1ms. Industry validation engagement with Steiner eOptics.
0.13 mrad
Current: Sub-0.7ms latency. Approaching and meeting DoD IVAS-grade performance targets.
